prompt:
You are a discerning poetry critic. Two poems follow, A and B. Exactly one is canonical, published verse; the other is weaker. Reply with only the letter of the better poem.

[A]
And on the pedestal these words appear
Who said two vast and trunkless legs of stone
Stand in the desert near them on the sand
Half sunk a shattered visage lies

[B]
Who said two vast and trunkless legs of stone
Stand in the desert near them on the sand
Half sunk a shattered visage lies
And on the pedestal these words appear

Which is the better poem, A or B? Answer with a single letter.

You are a discerning poetry critic. Two poems follow, A and B. Exactly one is canonical, published verse; the other is weaker. Reply with only the letter of the better poem.

[A]
Within the walls of glass, the sun's warm breath,
Cradles the growth of seeds in gentle heat.
Each leaf a story, life from tiny seed,
Bloomed bright and bold, without a single threat.

[B]
Rage rage against the dying of the light
The woods are lovely dark and deep
But I have promises to keep
And miles to go before I sleep

Which is the better poem, A or B? Answer with a single letter.

[A]
In hollows dark where dawn doth never enter,
The quarry's edge doth echo labor's song,
From stone and mountain, carvers shape their blunter,
Defying earth where giants dare to wrong.

[B]

Or bends with the remover to remove
That time of year thou mayst in me behold
When yellow leaves or none or few do hang
